title: PostgreSQL Metadata, Outbox Events, and Orphan Repair · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A production board compares near-miss TypeScript and AWS decisions for postgresql metadata, outbox events, and orphan repair and accepts only runtime validation, bounded ownership, causal changed-case evidence, and an explicit controlled AWS transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for typed metadata and object reconciliation model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, assign authoritative fields to application database, S3 object state, tags, events, or derived indexes and document reconciliation.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, persist idempotent intent, reservation, object identity, expected integrity, upload, quarantine, publish, failure, and expiry transitions.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, choose database-first, object-first, or direct-upload ordering and expose each crash window, compensation, and user-visible state.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript System Of Record Boundary · changed case · Quiz System Of Record Bou…
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 7464 during a private media entitlement change. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to expose PostgreSQL and S3 crash windows through an exhaustive workflow-state result; bound bytes, time, attempts, promises, streams, concurrency, cache, and spend; then double concurrent Upload instances and preserve aggregate memory and worker budgets. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, assign authoritative fields to application database, S3 object state, tags, events, or derived indexes and document reconciliation.
